keyMetrics.liquidity: ~$3.2B total liquidity; ~$4.7B pro forma after April raise

productionGuidance: Reaffirmed full-year 2026 production guidance of 25,000-27,000 vehicles in the production/deliveries 8-K, then on the earnings call suspended formal guidance pending the incoming CEO's strategic review. Q1 deliveries were hampered when Gravity shipments were disrupted for ~29 days by a supplier quality issue with the second-row seats; management said the issue has been resolved. The gap between 5,500 produced and 3,093 delivered drove an inventory build during the quarter.
demandCommentary: Management attributed the delivery shortfall to a ~29-day supplier-driven halt on Gravity shipments (second-row seat quality issue) rather than soft demand, and said the issue was addressed during the quarter. Production rose 149% YoY as the Gravity SUV ramp continued, and the company expanded its robotaxi partnership with Uber to at least 35,000 vehicles (Gravity plus the upcoming Midsize platform), signaling demand confidence in the ramp.
aiCommentary: Lucid is pursuing new revenue streams through autonomy, robotaxi, and software-enabled innovation, with the upcoming Midsize platform positioned to enable autonomous mobility at scale through cost efficiency and a technology-forward user experience. On margins, GAAP gross margin was deeply negative (~-110%), which management and coverage attributed to lower delivery volume against a largely fixed manufacturing cost base, under-absorption of fixed costs, and the non-repeat of large Q4 regulatory-credit revenue; the CFO framed the priority as improving operating leverage as the company scales.

keyQuotes.quote: First quarter results demonstrated the strength of our technology and product portfolio, and we are executing with focus across operations.
keyQuotes.speaker: Marc Winterhoff, Interim CEO

keyQuotes.quote: We strengthened our balance sheet and aim to improve operating leverage as we scale.
keyQuotes.speaker: Taoufiq Boussaid, CFO

sources.name: Lucid Group Form 8-K Exhibit 99.1: Q1 FY2026 Earnings Release (SEC EDGAR)
CNBC: Lucid suspends production guidance amid incoming CEO's business review (Q1 2026)
